name: Urolithin A
summary: Gut-microbiome-derived postbiotic metabolite and direct supplement tested as a mitophagy activator in preclinical aging models and human muscle or immune-aging trials.
mechanism_summary: Supplies a microbial ellagitannin metabolite directly, bypassing variable endogenous production, and promotes mitochondrial quality-control pathways with human studies measuring mitochondrial gene/protein signatures, acylcarnitines, inflammatory markers, muscle endurance, and immune-cell metabolism.

open_questions: Whether direct urolithin A supplementation should be interpreted primarily as microbiome-metabolite replacement, mitophagy activation, or both
Whether mitophagy engagement is the causal driver of the observed human muscle and immune signals
Whether benefits persist beyond 4 weeks to 4 months or translate into durable mobility, frailty, infection, disease, or mortality outcomes
How baseline microbiome metabolite-producing capacity changes response to direct supplementation or synbiotic precursor strategies
